Assistant Engineers ... ..
The pension in no case to exceed one-third of the emoluments drawn by the officer when invalided.

The periods of service mentioned in the foregoing table are inclusive of the periods of furlough allowed to count towards pension.

6. The following extra pension will be granted to an officer of the Indian Marine retiring after five years' service as Deputy Director or Director:—

Per annum.
For five years' continuous service as Director ... .. Rs. 2,000
Per annum.
For five years' continuous service as Deputy Director ... .. Rs. 1,000
For five years' continuous service, partly as Deputy Director and partly as Director, of which not less than two years shall have been as Director... .. Rs. 1,500

7. Service in the survey, or in port or dockyard appointments, gives no claim to any higher position than that fixed for any officer's rank in the Marine. Officers not on the Marine list now serving in such appointments will remain under their present rules.

8. The pensions of warrant officers, gunners, and clerks will be calculated under the rules for the Uncovenanted Service, as laid down in the Civil Service Regulations, Rs. 60 per mensem (compensation in lieu of board and lodging) being added to their pay for the calculation of pension.

9. Pensions will be payable in rupees in India. If paid at the Home Treasury to residents in the United Kingdom or to residents in

any other country in which the standard of currency is gold, they will be converted with effect from 1st April, 1890, at the rate of exchange annually fixed for the adjustment of financial transactions between the Imperial and Indian Governments, subject to a minimum rate of 1s. 9d. per rupee.

10. An officer may be allowed to commute a portion, not exceeding one-third of his pension, the commuted value being payable in India under the rules in Clause 23, India Army Circulars, of 1834.

11. Compassionate allowance on the following scale will be granted to the families of officers who may be drowned or meet with a violent death in the execution of duty:—

Widow. Child.
Commander ... .. } £100 a year, or Rs. 100 a month } £20 a year, or Rs. 20 a month.
Port Officer ... ..
Chief Engineer ... .. } £75 a year, or Rs. 75 a month. } £15 a year, or Rs. 15 a month.
Lieutenant... ..
Engineer ... .. } £50 a year, or Rs. 50 a month. } £10 a year, or Rs. 10 a month.
Sub-Lieutenant ... ..
Assistant Engineer ... ..

The cases of the families of the Director or Deputy Director will be specially considered.

12. The regulations and conditions under which all such compassionate allowances are to be awarded will be analogous to those prescribed for the military service.

Officers and others who have not elected the above rules or who are not eligible for them, and who are entitled to pension, will be granted pensions and gratuities under the rules contained in the Civil Service Regulations.

INDIAN NURSING SERVICE.

CONDITIONS OF APPOINTMENT, &c.

1. The Nursing Establishment consists of two grades, viz. :—

  1. (1.) Lady Superintendents.
  2. (2.) Nursing Sisters.

Note.—The term "lady nurse" as used in the following paragraphs includes both the above grades.

2. Nursing Sisters must be, at the time of appointment, over 25 and under 35 years of age, and before admission to the service they must be examined by the President of the Medical Board at the India Office as to their fitness for service in India. They must have had at least three years' preliminary training and service combined in a hospital in which adult male patients receive medical and surgical treatment, and in which a staff of Nursing Sisters is maintained.

3. The duration of a term of service, for all grades of lady nurses, is five years, capable of renewal on a further five years at the option of

the Government, with the consent of the lady nurse.

A lady nurse pronounced by a Medical Board to be physically fit for further service in India, and who may be specially recommended by the Commander-in-Chief India for an extension of service, will be permitted to serve for a third term, and, in very exceptional cases, for a fourth term of five years.

4. The engagement may, however, be terminated at any time on six months' notice being given, either on the part of the Government or of the lady nurse (but see para. 12).

Rates of Pay.

(In addition to free quarters, fuel, light, and punkah-pullers.)

  1. 5. For a Lady Superintendent ... .. 300 per mensem.
  2. For a Nursing Sister ... .. 175 "

INDIAN NURSING SERVICE—continued.

commencing from the date of embarkation for India.

A Senior Nursing Sister acting as Superintendent at a station where no lady superintendent is located, receives an additional allowance of Rs. 25 per mensem.

Leave in India.

6. Leave on medical certificate, up to a maximum of six months during the whole of the five years' term of service, may be granted to a lady nurse by the Principal Medical Officer of Her Majesty's Forces; during such leave, which will reckon as service, she will receive two-thirds of the salary of her grade. Short leave and privilege leave may be granted at the discretion

of the local authorities and as circumstances may admit.

Leave out of India.

7. After engagement for a second, third, or fourth term of service, a lady nurse may be granted one year's leave from duty, on two-thirds pay, with free passage by sea and rail from and to her station. This leave may be extended, on medical certificate, on two-thirds pay up to two years' absence from duty: provided that in the opinion of the India Office Medical Board there is a reasonable prospect of fitness to return to duty within that period. Such period will not reckon as service in any way.

Gratuities.

8. The following rates of gratuities are payable to lady nurses on completion of their total Indian service:—

Deputy Superintendent or Nursing Sister. Lady Superintendent.
For a completed term of five years ... .. Rs.
500		 As a nursing sister, with Rs. 140 additional for each complete year's service as lady superintendent.
For a completed term of ten years ... .. 1,500 As a nursing sister, with Rs. 250 additional for each complete year's service as lady superintendent.
If compelled by sickness to leave India before completion of a five years' term, for each complete year's service ... .. 75 Rs.
200
If compelled by sickness to leave India after completion of a first term of five years' service, but before completion of a second, for each complete year of the second five years' term, and in addition to the gratuity for the completed term of five years as shown above 100 240

9. No gratuity is given for service terminated by any other cause than sickness or the completion of the terms of 5 and 10 years' service.

Pensions.

10. Lady nurses retiring after completion of 15 or 20 years' total service will be entitled to pension at the following rates:—

Nursing Sister, per annum. Lady Superintendent.
After 15 years' service ... £
50		 As Nursing Sister, with £2 additional for each completed year's service in the grade of Lady Superintendent.
" 20 " " ... 60

11. A lady nurse compelled by ill-health to retire after 15 but under 20 years' total service will receive a pension of £50 a year, with an increment of £2 for each completed year over 15 years' service, and an additional £2 a year, in the case of a Lady Superintendent, for the total number of years served in that grade.

fixed for 15 years' service, as may be determined by the Secretary of State for India in Council on the recommendation of the Government of India.

12. A lady nurse compelled by ill-health to retire after 10, but under 15 years' service will be granted such rate of pension, below that

13. If paid in India, these pensions will be payable in Indian currency at the rate of exchange annually fixed for the adjustment of transactions between the Imperial and Indian Governments.

Passage, &c.

14. Passage at the public expense, subject to a deduction of 2s. a day for messing while on board ship, is granted to lady nurses when proceeding to India on appointment, when returning home on leave (under paragraph 7) on completion of a term of service, or if invalided home before the completion of a term. They are entitled to take 6 cwt. of baggage. Lady nurses are also entitled to travelling expenses on the above occasions from their places of residence in England to port of embarkation, and from port of disembarkation in India to destination in that country, and vice versa.

Claims for conveyance of baggage must be supported by vouchers. Agency and dock charges are inadmissible.

15. An outfit allowance on appointment is made at the rate of £25 for each Lady Superintendent, and £15 for each Nursing Sister.

16. A lady nurse who resigns (except on account of ill-health), with less than five years' Indian service, will forthwith refund the sum of £20 in respect of her passage out (or £30 if she have failed to give the notice required by Rule 4), and will not be entitled to a passage home.

17. A lady nurse who has re-engaged for a second term of service in India will be subject to the same penalties if she resigns (except on account of ill-health) without giving six months' notice, but not otherwise.

18. Lady nurses must be prepared to embark, if necessary, not later than 30 days from date of appointment.
